steps.title: Locate the two independent clauses
steps.content: Clause 1 ends at "silkworm silk": "Researcher Lin Zhi developed a process for increasing the tensile strength…of silkworm silk." Clause 2 is "Zhi increased the amount of force required to stretch it from approximately 0.5 GPa to 2 GPa," preceded by the introductory phrase "By dissolving and reweaving the silk in a solution of iron metal ions, zinc, and sugar."

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, a semicolon, or a comma plus a coordinating conjunction. A period followed by a capitalized "By" creates a clean break and lets the participial phrase modify the new clause.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'silk, by': a comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'silk by': zero punctuation creates a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — 'silk and by': "and" is not preceded by a comma, and the resulting structure is ungrammatical because "by dissolving…Zhi increased…" is not parallel to anything on the left. ✗
  • Choice D — 'silk. By': a period correctly separates the two independent clauses. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
